title: andropogon mohrii
intro: Andropogon mohrii, also known as Buffalo grass, is a type of perennial grass native to North America, characterized by its short, fine blades and tolerance to drought and poor soil conditions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Andropogon mohrii include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer and antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of Andropogon mohrii can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support immune function and overall well-being.
